text: What a remarkable thing you've done. A marathon is not really won at the finish line; it is won in all the quiet, unglamorous mornings that came before, the miles no one witnessed, the small refusals to quit. To cross that line for the first time is to discover that the body and the will, when patient with each other, can carry you further than you believed. So let yourself feel it fully. You set out to do something difficult and lasting, and you did it. The medal is lovely, but the truer prize is the knowledge you now hold of yourself, unshakable and earned. Rest well, ache proudly, and remember this feeling. Congratulations, marathoner. You have every reason to be deeply, quietly proud.
